p: Let $ A = \begin{bmatrix} \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} & -2 \\ 0 & 1 \end{bmatrix} $ and $ P = \begin{bmatrix} \cos \theta & -\sin \theta \\ \sin \theta & \cos \theta \end{bmatrix}, \theta \geq 0. $ If $ B = PAP^T, C = P^TB^TP $ and the sum of the diagonal elements of $ C $ is $ \frac{m}{n} $, where $ \gcd(m, n) = 1 $, then $ m + n $ is:

If the distance of the point $ C $ from the line bisecting the angle between the vectors $ \overrightarrow{OA} $ and $ \overrightarrow{OB} $ is $ \frac{a}{\sqrt{2}} $, then the sum of all the possible values of $ a $ is:
